summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates process.env to attacker-controlled Azure blob via custom TLS client at postinstall/require
details: The package impersonates the Electron and OrbitDB ecosystems (name 'electron-orbit', author 'OrbitDB Archive', homepage pointing at github.com/orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron) while shipping a covert env-var exfiltration payload. install.js is wired as a postinstall script (package.json line 47: "postinstall": "node install.js") and, using an XOR string-decoding routine to hide the identifiers 'process' and 'env' from static scanners, enumerates the installer's environment variables (filtering out keys containing 'path') and writes the dump to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. dist/index.js, at top-level module load, reads that marker file, XOR-encodes it with the key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes the result, and transmits it as a query-string parameter to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net/icons/orbit.png. The transport is a from-scratch TLS 1.3 client (ClientHello/ServerHello/HKDF/AES-128-GCM) implemented over a raw node:net socket rather than the standard https module — the destination host, path, and module name 'node:net' are all XOR-obfuscated. Activation of the real exfil URL is gated by a SHA256 check on process.env.BuildType matching a hardcoded digest (0ceaa396e2134a9937563e841539f30a9995e6e8646e60dcec24b26dc5748295), keeping the payload dormant on incidental installers and firing in the targeted build environment. Installer environment variables in CI/build contexts routinely contain credentials, tokens, and API keys; this is install-time / import-time credential theft with deliberate evasion (custom TLS to bypass observable https, XOR-hidden identifiers, target-environment trigger).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
